learningGoal: A brilliant shortcut: the FIRST letter of word 1 is often the one that moves!
How to quickly test this by covering the front letter — it takes just seconds

screens.title: Start at the start!
screens.body:
Daisy is working on letter-move puzzles at home.

Here's a great shortcut: the **first letter** of word 1 is often the one that moves. It's the easiest to test — just chop it off the front and see what's left!

**BLAND** and **OAR** — what happens if you remove the first letter?

visualProps.label: BLAND  →  move a letter  →  OAR

screens.title: Chop the front — see what's hiding!
screens.body: Look at **BLAND** and **OAR**. Try this: cover up the first letter of BLAND with your finger. Can you read a real word underneath? If YES, you might have found the answer already!

interaction.correctFeedback: Perfect! Cover, check, insert, check — that's the system. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite — start by covering the first letter, then check both new words.

steps.why: Is LAND a real word? YES!
Is BOAR a real word? YES!

steps.text: Both LAND and BOAR are real ✓
steps.why: The first letter was the answer!

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
Which letter moves from **FLAME** to **OWL**?

Try the first letter first!

interaction.question: Which letter should you move from FLAME to OWL?
interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! Remove F from FLAME = LAME (unable to walk properly). Insert F at the start of OWL = FOWL (a bird). Both are real words! ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "F". Remove F from FLAME = LAME (unable to walk properly). Insert F at the start of OWL = FOWL (a bird). Both are real words! ✓

visualProps.label: Move a letter from FLAME into OWL:

screens.title: First letter first — easy wins!
screens.body: You've got a great starting strategy now! When you see a letter-move question:

steps.text: 1. Try the FIRST letter of word 1
steps.why: Just cover it up — is the rest a real word?

steps.text: 2. If it's real, try inserting that letter into word 2
steps.why: Usually at the start of word 2
The first letter is the answer more often than you'd think ✓

learningGoal: What to do when the first letter trick doesn't work — don't panic, just try the next!
How to stay calm and move through options without wasting time

screens.title: First letter of SHOUT — does it work?
screens.body:
Evie is checking her practice answers.

You'd normally try the **first letter** — but look what happens with **SHOUT** and **PEN**:

Remove "S" from SHOUT → **HOUT** — not a real word! Don't worry — just move on to the next letter. You've got this!

visualProps.label: SHOUT  →  move a letter  →  PEN

screens.title: When the first letter fails — try the next!
screens.body: The first letter "S" didn't work: SHOUT → HOUT (not a word!). So we move to the **next letter** and keep testing:

steps.why: Not a real word — move on!
SHUT IS a real word! Keep going...
OPEN is a real word too! ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body: Which letter moves from **GROWN** to **ATE**?

interaction.question: Which letter should you move from GROWN to ATE?
interaction.correctFeedback: Well done! Remove G? ROWN isn't a word! Try R: GROWN → GOWN (a long dress). Insert R into ATE → RATE (a speed). Both real words! ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "R". Remove G? ROWN isn't a word! Try R: GROWN → GOWN (a long dress). Insert R into ATE → RATE (a speed). Both real words! ✓

visualProps.label: Move a letter from GROWN into ATE:

screens.title: No stress — just try the next one!
screens.body: The first letter is a great starting point, but it won't always be the answer. No problem! Here's your plan:

steps.why: Is the rest a real word? If YES, great — test it with word 2

steps.text: If NOT a real word, don't panic!
steps.why: Move on to the second letter, then the third, and so on

steps.text: Keep testing until BOTH new words work
steps.why: The answer is often the 2nd or last letter when the 1st fails ✓

learningGoal: Another quick test: sometimes it's the LAST letter that needs to move!
Why checking the end is just as fast as checking the front

screens.title: Don't forget the end!
screens.body:
Daisy is trying a new set of letter-move questions.

If the first letter doesn't work, try the **last letter** next! It's just as quick — cover the end of the word and see what's left. Easy!

**PLANT** — what happens if you remove the last letter?

visualProps.label: PLANT  →  move a letter  →  OUR

screens.title: Cover the end — read what's left
screens.body: Look at **PLANT** and **OUR**. Put your finger over the last letter of PLANT. Can you read a real word? If YES, test inserting that letter into word 2.

steps.why: Is PLAN a real word? YES!
Is TOUR a real word? YES!

steps.text: PLAN ✓ and TOUR ✓ — the last letter was the answer!
steps.why: Always check BOTH new words

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
Which letter moves from **JOINT** to **OUR**?

Try the last letter!

interaction.question: Which letter should you move from JOINT to OUR?
interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! Remove T from the end of JOINT = JOIN (to connect). Insert T at the start of OUR = TOUR (a journey). Both are real words! ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "T". Remove T from the end of JOINT = JOIN (to connect). Insert T at the start of OUR = TOUR (a journey). Both are real words! ✓

visualProps.label: Move a letter from JOINT into OUR:

screens.title: Two quick tests in your pocket!
screens.body: Brilliant — now you've got TWO fast checks. The first and last letters are the easiest:

steps.why: Cover the front — read the rest

steps.text: 2. If that doesn't work, try the LAST letter
steps.why: Cover the end — read what's left
Only go to middle letters if neither works ✓

learningGoal: The trickier cases: sometimes the letter hiding in the MIDDLE is the one that moves!
Why trying first and last before the middle keeps you fast and organised

screens.title: Sometimes it's hiding in the middle!
screens.body:
Lily is stuck on a tricky letter-move question.

OK, so the first and last letters didn't work. No worries — sometimes the letter that moves is **hiding in the middle** of word 1. These are the trickiest ones, but you can handle them!

**GRAVE** and **OAR** — which middle letter makes two real words?

visualProps.label: GRAVE  →  move a letter  →  OAR

screens.title: Pull out the middle letter
screens.body: Look at **GRAVE** and **OAR**. When you remove a middle letter from GRAVE, the letters on EITHER SIDE close up. They stay in order!

steps.why: The other letters close the gap
Is GAVE a real word? YES!
ROAR is a real word too! ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
Which letter moves from **PLANE** to **ATE**?

Hint: it's NOT the first or last letter!

interaction.question: Which letter should you move from PLANE to ATE?
interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! Remove L from PLANE = PANE (a sheet of glass). Insert L at the start of ATE = LATE (not on time). Both are real words! ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "L". Remove L from PLANE = PANE (a sheet of glass). Insert L at the start of ATE = LATE (not on time). Both are real words! ✓

visualProps.label: Move a letter from PLANE into ATE:

screens.title: Middle letters — you can handle these!
screens.body: Middle letters are the trickiest, so save them for last. But now you know how:

steps.why: Quickest check
Second quickest
When you remove a middle letter, the sides close up ✓

subConcepts.name: Adding the Letter to the Front

learningGoal: Where does the letter land? Start position is the most common — and easiest to test!
Why trying start-insertion first gives you the best chance of a quick answer

screens.title: Where does the letter land?
screens.body:
Evie is learning where to put the moved letter.

Once you've found the letter to move, the next question is: **where does it go** in word 2? Good news — the most common spot is right at the **START**! Try that first.

**SPINE** → move "S" → pop it at the start of **OAR**.

visualProps.label: SPINE  →  move a letter  →  OAR

screens.title: Start position — the most common
screens.body: We've found that **"S"** moves from **SPINE**. Where does it go in **OAR**? In most letter-move questions, the moved letter goes to the **front** of word 2. Always try the start first!

interaction.sentence: In most letter-move questions, the moved letter goes to the ____ of word 2
interaction.correctFeedback: Yes! The start is the most common insertion position — always try it first. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite — in most cases, the moved letter goes to the START of word 2!

steps.why: Removed from SPINE to make PINE
Is SOAR a real word? YES!

steps.text: Both PINE and SOAR are real ✓
steps.why: Start position worked!

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body: Which letter moves from **CRATE** to the START of **OAT**?

interaction.question: Which letter should you move from CRATE to OAT?
interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! Remove C from CRATE = RATE (a speed or level). Insert C at the START of OAT = COAT (a jacket). Both are real words! ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "C". Remove C from CRATE = RATE (a speed or level). Insert C at the START of OAT = COAT (a jacket). Both are real words! ✓

visualProps.label: Move a letter from CRATE into OAT:

screens.title: Start position — your best bet!
screens.body: Great work! When inserting the moved letter into word 2, try the start first:

steps.text: 1. Try the START of word 2 first
steps.why: Put the letter right at the front

steps.text: 2. Read the new word — is it real?
steps.why: Say it out loud if you need to
S + OAR = SOAR, B + OWL = BOWL, T + OWN = TOWN ✓

subConcepts.name: Adding the Letter to the End

learningGoal: The backup plan: if the start doesn't work, try putting the letter at the END!
Why having two positions to check (start and end) means you'll always find the answer

screens.title: Plot twist — it goes at the end!
screens.body:
Daisy is discovering the letter can go at the end.

Usually the moved letter goes at the **start** of word 2. But sometimes it goes at the **END** instead! If the front doesn't make a real word, just flip to the back. Easy switch!

visualProps.label: SHAVE  →  move a letter  →  EAR

screens.title: Try the end when the start fails
screens.body: Look at **SHAVE** and **EAR**. If putting the letter at the front of word 2 doesn't make a real word, try putting it at the **end** instead.

steps.why: HAVE is a real word!
Does it make a real word? Maybe not...
EARS IS a real word! ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body:
Which letter moves from **PLANT** to **BEA**?

Remember — the letter might go at the END!

interaction.question: Which letter should you move from PLANT to BEA?
interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! Remove T from the end of PLANT = PLAN (a scheme). Insert T at the END of BEA = BEAT (to win against). Both are real words! ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "T". Remove T from the end of PLANT = PLAN (a scheme). Insert T at the END of BEA = BEAT (to win against). Both are real words! ✓

visualProps.label: Move a letter from PLANT into BEA:

screens.title: Start or end — now you can try both!
screens.body: Brilliant! You've got two places to check when placing the moved letter:

steps.text: 1. Try the letter at the START first
steps.why: This works most of the time

steps.text: 2. If that's not a word, try the END
steps.why: HIS + S = HISS, HEAR + T = HEART

steps.text: 3. Check BOTH new words are real
steps.why: Don't forget to verify word 1 too ✓

learningGoal: Don't forget the vowels! Sometimes A, E, I, O or U is the letter that moves
Why vowel moves can surprise you — the word looks totally different without them

screens.title: Surprise — vowels move too!
screens.body:
Evie is tackling a vowel-move question.

**Did you know?** Don't just test consonants (B, C, D...) — sometimes a **vowel** (A, E, I, O, U) is the letter that moves! Vowel moves can be surprising because the word looks totally different without its vowel.

visualProps.label: HOUSE  →  move a letter  →  NIT

screens.title: Testing vowel removal
screens.body: Look at **HOUSE** and **NIT**. When you remove a vowel from a word, the remaining letters can look very odd. Say it aloud — does it sound like a real word?

steps.why: "U" is a vowel
Is HOSE a real word? YES!
UNIT is a real word too ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body: Which **vowel** moves from **HOUSE** to **NIT**?

interaction.question: Which letter should you move from HOUSE to NIT?
interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! Remove U from HOUSE = HOSE (a tube for water). Insert U at the start of NIT = UNIT (a single item). Both are real words! ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "U". Remove U from HOUSE = HOSE (a tube for water). Insert U at the start of NIT = UNIT (a single item). Both are real words! ✓

visualProps.label: Move a letter from HOUSE into NIT:

screens.title: Vowels — now in your toolkit!
screens.body: Nice one! Now you know vowels can move too. Here's what to remember:

steps.text: Vowels: A, E, I, O, U
steps.why: Any of these can be the moved letter

steps.text: Remove the vowel and say what's left out loud
steps.why: Does it sound like a real word?

steps.text: HOUSE - U = HOSE, FLOAT - O = FLAT
steps.why: Vowel moves are real — test them ✓

learningGoal: Meet the usual suspects: the consonants that move the most (B, C, S, G, P, F, T)
How recognising these common movers gives you a head start

screens.title: Meet the usual suspects!
screens.body:
Daisy is looking for which consonant moves.

**Did you know?** Consonants are the letters that move most often, and some are real favourites: **S, B, P, G, F** and **C**. Spot one of these at the start or end of word 1? That's a great place to start testing!

**GRIPE** and **LOW** — which consonant moves?

visualProps.label: GRIPE  →  move a letter  →  LOW

screens.title: The usual suspects: S, B, P, G, F, C
screens.body: **GRIPE** starts with **"G"** — is that one of the usual suspects? The consonants S, B, P, G, F, and C are the most commonly moved letters. If word 1 starts with one of them, test it first!

steps.why: Is "G" one of the usual suspects?
RIPE is a real word!
GLOW is a real word too ✓

screens.title: Your turn!
screens.body: Which consonant moves from **SWEAR** to **POT**?

interaction.question: Which letter should you move from SWEAR to POT?
interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! Remove S from SWEAR = WEAR (to put on clothes). Insert S at the start of POT = SPOT (a mark or place). Both are real words! ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "S". Remove S from SWEAR = WEAR (to put on clothes). Insert S at the start of POT = SPOT (a mark or place). Both are real words! ✓

visualProps.label: Move a letter from SWEAR into POT:

screens.title: Your consonant cheat sheet
screens.body: Excellent! Keep these common movers in mind — they'll save you time:

steps.why: S is the #1 most common mover
B is very common at the start

steps.text: P, G, F, C: PRICE→RICE, GLOVE→LOVE, FLAKE→LAKE
steps.why: These are all worth testing first ✓

learningGoal: A proper system for testing letters: first, last, then middle — no more guessing!
Why having this system means you'll never feel stuck or panicky in the exam

screens.title: Work smart — use a system!
screens.body:
Evie is using a system to find the answer quickly.

Here's the thing that separates good solvers from great ones: having a **system** instead of guessing randomly.

1. Try the **first** letter
2. Try the **last** letter
3. Then try **middle** letters

This order is fastest because first and last letters are the quickest to test!

visualProps.label: CREAM  →  move a letter  →  OAT

screens.title: First → Last → Middle
screens.body: Let's work through **CREAM** and **OAT** step by step:

interaction.correctFeedback: Perfect! First → last → middle is the fastest system. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite — always try first letter, then last, then middle.

steps.why: Remove it: does the rest make a word?
Cover the end — is the rest real?
System found it! ✓

screens.title: Your turn — use the system!
screens.body:
Follow the system: first → last → middle.

Which letter moves from **CREAM** to **OAT**?

interaction.question: Which letter should you move from CREAM to OAT?
interaction.correctFeedback: Well done! Test the first letter: remove C from CREAM = REAM (a quantity of paper). Insert C at the start of OAT = COAT (a jacket). Both are real words! ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "C". Test the first letter: remove C from CREAM = REAM (a quantity of paper). Insert C at the start of OAT = COAT (a jacket). Both are real words! ✓

visualProps.label: Move a letter from CREAM into OAT:

screens.title: Your testing system — locked in!
screens.body: Fantastic! You've got a proper system now. Always test in this order:

steps.text: 1. FIRST letter — cover it, read the rest
steps.why: Takes 2 seconds to check

steps.text: 2. LAST letter — cover the end, read what's left
steps.why: Also takes 2 seconds

steps.text: 3. MIDDLE letters — only if 1 and 2 fail
steps.why: First + last solves most questions ✓

learningGoal: The golden rule: BOTH new words must be real — one out of two won't do!
How to dodge the sneaky traps where one word looks real but the other isn't

screens.title: Both words MUST be real!
screens.body:
Daisy is learning to double-check both words.

Watch out for the biggest trap in letter-move questions: sometimes removing a letter makes word 1 look fine, but the new word 2 **isn't a real word at all**.

Always check **BOTH** words before you commit to your answer!

visualProps.label: SPARK  →  move a letter  →  OAK

screens.title: The double-check rule
screens.body:
Let's check **SPARK** and **OAK**. After removing a letter, ask TWO questions:
1. Is the new word 1 real?
2. Is the new word 2 real?

If EITHER answer is no, try a different letter!

interaction.sentence: After moving a letter, you must check that ____ new words are real
interaction.correctFeedback: Yes! Both new words must be real English words. ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite — BOTH new words must be real for the answer to be correct!

steps.why: Is PARK a real word? YES ✓
Is SOAK a real word? YES ✓

steps.text: BOTH are real — this is the answer!
steps.why: Never submit until you've checked both ✓

screens.title: Your turn — check both!
screens.body: Which letter moves from **BRACE** to **OAT** to make **TWO** real words?

interaction.question: Which letter should you move from BRACE to OAT?
interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! Remove B from BRACE = RACE (a competition). Insert B at the start of OAT = BOAT (floats on water). RACE ✓ and BOAT ✓ — both real! ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "B". Remove B from BRACE = RACE (a competition). Insert B at the start of OAT = BOAT (floats on water). RACE ✓ and BOAT ✓ — both real! ✓

visualProps.label: Move a letter from BRACE into OAT:

screens.title: The BOTH rule — your safety net!
screens.body: You've learned the golden rule that keeps you safe from traps:

steps.why: Is what's left a REAL word?
Is the new word 2 REAL?

steps.text: 3. BOTH must be real — or try again!
steps.why: One real + one fake = WRONG answer ✓

learningGoal: The one-word trap: it looks right but only ONE new word is real — gotcha!
Why taking an extra second to check saves you from losing easy marks

screens.title: Trap alert: "L" looks tempting!
screens.body:
Evie is finding the mistake in her friend's answer.

Someone picked **"L"** as the letter to move from **BLEND** to **OAR**.

Remove "L" → BEND (looks OK!)
Insert "L" → LOAR (is that a word...?)

BEND is real, but LOAR isn't a word! You must check BOTH.

visualProps.label: BLEND  →  move a letter  →  OAR

screens.title: The trap — one real, one fake
screens.body:
BEND is real, but LOAR isn't a word! You must check BOTH.

The correct letter is **"B"** — let's check BOTH words:

steps.why: LEND — real word? YES ✓
BOAR — real word? YES ✓

steps.text: BOTH are real — this is correct!
steps.why: Always verify both before answering ✓

screens.title: Your turn — avoid the trap!
screens.body:
Which letter ACTUALLY moves from **STALE** to **OAR**?

Remember to check BOTH new words are real!

interaction.question: Which letter should you move from STALE to OAR?
interaction.correctFeedback: Brilliant! Remove S from STALE = TALE (a story). Insert S at the start of OAR = SOAR (to fly high). TALE ✓ and SOAR ✓ — both real! ✓
interaction.incorrectFeedback: Not quite! The answer is "S". Remove S from STALE = TALE (a story). Insert S at the start of OAR = SOAR (to fly high). TALE ✓ and SOAR ✓ — both real! ✓

visualProps.label: Move a letter from STALE into OAR:

screens.title: Trap-proof — you've got this!
screens.body: Now you know the sneakiest trap in letter-move questions — and how to avoid it:

steps.why: SOLD is real, but COWN isn't!

steps.text: Or checking word 2 but forgetting word 1
steps.why: Both must be real English words

steps.text: ALWAYS check BOTH before you answer
steps.why: Two ticks needed: word 1 ✓ AND word 2 ✓
